title: Non-record: Depth Recurrence in Parameter-Constrained Transformers — What Works, What Doesn't, and Why
author: evangelinehelsinki

data.description: Shares transformer block weights across multiple cycles to create more effective depth within the parameter budget.
Hashes consecutive token pairs into embedding buckets to add bigram context.
Exclusive Self Attention applied on the last virtual layers to reduce self-value bias.
Per-virtual-layer low-rank adapters to let shared blocks specialize across cycles.
Additional gating component tested in ablations; reported to hurt with recurrence.
Uses rotary positional embeddings only partially in the architecture.
Grouped-query attention / reduced KV heads in the transformer blocks.

data.scope: shared/recycled weights int8, single-use tensors int6
late quantization-aware training on the full pipeline

novel_contributions: Depth recurrence / weight sharing across transformer blocks to fit more effective depth into the parameter budget
Noisy QAT: differentiable uniform noise matching int8 quantization error on recurrent core blocks
Empirical finding that quantization error compounds strongly through recurrence, motivating mixed-precision storage
Ablation result that 3x3 recurrent layouts outperform 2x5 loops in this setting
BigramHash as a strong additive feature for recurrent transformers
Documented negative results across multiple attempted techniques and hyperparameter variants
